name: Heureka, Vantaa
concept&appearance: Designed by Heikkinen-Komonen Architects (Mikko Heikkinen and Markku Komonen), the building represents a fusion of minimalism, high-technology, and abstract expressionism. The design concept breaks the facility into distinct geometric volumes (cylindrical hall, pillar hall, wedge-shaped auditorium) that intersect. The exterior features a 100-meter-long glass facade facing the railway, which acts as a noise barrier and displays a color spectrum structure representing the physics of light (31 parts corresponding to spectral colors). The materials include stark white concrete, steel, and mirror glass, creating a 'mathematical sculpture' that fits into the landscape near the Keravanjoki River.
permanent_exhibitions: 1. Main Exhibition Area (Cylindrical Hall & Pillar Hall): Features the 'Heureka Classics', a collection of long-standing interactive exhibits focusing on fundamental physics, illusions, and perception (e.g., the wire-walking bicycle, flying cars). 2. The Idea Workshop: A hands-on maker space where visitors design and build solutions to challenges (e.g., building cars or boats). 3. Science on a Sphere: A spherical projection system displaying planetary data, ocean currents, and climate models. 4. Intelligent City (Electri City) & Circular Factory: Long-term exhibitions focusing on smart power grids, recycling, and sustainable development. 5. Rottala (Rat Basketball): A unique, long-running exhibit where trained rats play basketball, demonstrating animal intelligence and conditioning. 6. Science Park Galilei: An outdoor exhibition area open during the summer, featuring water experiments, mathematical puzzles, and the bedrock exhibition displaying Finnish geological specimens.
central_hall: The architectural focus point is the Cylindrical Exhibition Hall, which is 14 meters tall. It connects to the Pillar Hall, a large open space defined by a grid of pillars (9.6m module) that visualizes load distribution.
special_effects_theaters: Planetarium: A digital hemispheric theater with 135 seats and a 17.5-meter dome. Originally the Verne Theatre with a superwide screen, it was converted to a digital planetarium. It uses Sky-Skan/RSA Cosmos technology for immersive space and science films. (Note: Closed for major renovation from late 2024 to late 2025).
science_popularization_activities: Heureka is famous for its 'Rat Basketball' shows, where rats play games to demonstrate behavioral science. The center offers daily science shows, laboratory workshops (e.g., chemistry experiments), summer science camps (including space camps), and extensive school programs utilizing the 'Science on a Sphere' data visualizations.
